title: Flatten a Multilevel Doubly Linked List
problem_statement: Flatten a multilevel doubly linked list so that all nodes appear in a single-level doubly linked list.

examples.explanation: Child lists are inserted into the parent list in depth-first order.

constraints: Number of nodes does not exceed 1000
intuition: Use DFS or a stack to traverse child pointers and splice children between nodes.

approaches.name: Iterative DFS flatten
approaches.description: Use a stack to visit nodes in depth-first order, rewiring next and prev pointers as you go.
approaches.why_not: Recursive DFS may use stack space proportional to depth, though it still works for moderate depths.

interview_qa.a: Push the next pointer onto a stack and process child pointers first so child nodes follow the parent.
The flattened list should not retain child references.
